transcripts.summary:
- **Successful survey launch confirmed:** 5-day survey data loaded first; 30-day data follows. 90-day survey deferred due to API issues.  
- **Manual audit reports created:** Ensuring survey participation is tracked and verified against Workday data weekly for data health.  
- **Pulse survey planned for April:** Enhancement prioritization in progress; effort estimates from Yugi’s team needed by tomorrow.  
- **Reminder frequency reduced:** Limit to one follow-up per survey; aim for better participant engagement by reducing annoyances.  
- **Dashboard enhancements requested:** Need for date filtering and cleaner driver visibility; updates on feasibility and timelines to follow.  
- **Survey links remain active:** Participants can respond anytime; reminders adjusted for 14-day open response window.

## **Survey Launch and Data Loading**

The onboarding surveys for the 5-day and 30-day periods are live, with data loading and audit processes underway to ensure accuracy.

- **Successful survey launch and phased data loading confirmed** (07:59)  
    - The 5-day survey data was loaded first, with the 30-day data scheduled immediately after the call.  
    - Manual data gathering was necessary to address system issues, successfully handled by Sonia and Kate.  
    - The 90-day survey data was deferred due to employees exceeding the threshold because of API downtime, pending internal discussion on inclusion.  
    - Management was already informed about the reversed launch order compared to the engagement survey.

- **Audit approach designed to verify survey participation** (08:54)  
    - Audit reports will be built manually since automated reports are not available, capturing who received and responded to surveys.  
    - Data will be compared against Workday records to ensure completeness.  
    - This auditing will be done weekly, with 100% participation indicating good data health to senior management.  
    - The confidential past survey data is securely stored in a private repository.

- **Clarified survey thresholds and inclusion criteria** (06:22)  
    - Employees above the 90-day threshold are currently excluded from the 90-day survey due to timing issues.  
    - Pulse surveys will include employees within the last 3 to 6 months, ensuring new employees are captured appropriately.  
    - The team decided to proceed with sending only 5-day and 30-day surveys for now, pending further internal alignment.

- **Project completion status and minor pending items** (11:13)  
    - The onboarding survey launch project is effectively 99% complete with only sub-admin access controls remaining.  
    - Sonia and Kate are monitoring lifecycle analytics and dashboards for further insights and training needs.

## **2026 Pulse Survey Planning and Enhancement Prioritization**

Preparation for the 2026 pulse survey is in progress, focusing on evaluating tool enhancements by prioritizing effort and feasibility for an April launch.

- **Pulse survey targeted for April with enhancement evaluation underway** (12:16)  
    - Charlie emphasized the need to balance enhancement priorities against available time and effort estimates.  
    - The team is awaiting effort estimates from Yugi’s team to finalize which enhancements can be realistically delivered.  
    - Effort estimates will be provided by the next morning to inform leadership prioritization discussions.

- **Clear definitions for enhancement status to guide decisions** (14:43)  
    - Accepted features are confirmed feasible, rejected ones are not possible currently, with some pending further discussion.  
    - Scott clarified that internal notes (columns H, L, M, N) are for team alignment and not part of Culture Monkey’s evaluation.  
    - Only the top 24 enhancement requests are under Culture Monkey’s review; remaining items require internal priority setting.

- **Level of effort estimates critical for realistic planning** (13:10)  
    - Charlie stressed that some high-priority enhancements may be deprioritized if effort exceeds available time.  
    - Yugi committed to delivering detailed time estimates, enabling leadership to make informed trade-offs.  
    - The team acknowledged some variability in estimates but expects them to be stable enough for planning.

- **No open questions from Culture Monkey before estimates delivery** (16:34)  
    - Yugi confirmed no additional clarifications needed before providing estimates.  
    - This ensures smooth handoff and alignment for the next phase of prioritization and scheduling.

## **Survey Reminder and Participation Management**

Adjustments to survey reminder frequency and participation tracking aim to improve employee experience and data quality.

- **Proposal to reduce survey reminders to a single follow-up** (22:36)  
    - Kate and Sonia suggested limiting reminders to one per survey, sent on day seven after initial delivery.  
    - This contrasts with the current default of weekly reminders up to 30 days, which may annoy participants.  
    - The team agreed a 14-day open response window with a single reminder fits employee engagement better.

- **Survey links remain active indefinitely but reminders will be adjusted** (24:38)  
    - Karthik clarified that survey links do not expire, allowing employees to respond at any time.  
    - The team will explore technical feasibility to limit reminders to 14 days as requested.  
    - Confirmation of these changes is expected within the current week.

- **Parallel surveys may cause overlapping reminders** (21:05)  
    - Employees receiving the 5-day survey become eligible for the 30-day survey by day 30, triggering overlapping notifications.  
    - Sonia raised concerns about potential survey fatigue due to simultaneous survey reminders.  
    - The reminder adjustment aims to mitigate this by reducing frequency and improving participant experience.

- **Participation and silent participant tracking clarified** (19:20)  
    - Silent participants are those who have not yet responded, counted from survey launch onward.  
    - Surveys remain open for 14 days with reminders sent weekly until 30 days, after which reminders stop but participants remain counted.  
    - This ongoing participation tracking helps maintain data completeness and highlights non-responders effectively.

## **Lifecycle Analytics and Dashboard Enhancements**

The team reviewed current lifecycle analytics capabilities and requested improvements for date filtering and driver visibility.

- **Current dashboards show rolling 7-day participation with limited date filtering** (18:33)  
    - Yugi explained lifecycle analytics dashboards display only the last 7 days of survey participation by default.  
    - Total participation data covering the entire survey period is available via report exports.  
    - Sonia requested ability to filter dashboard data by custom date ranges to track trends over time.

- **Limitations on applying date filters due to continuous survey nature** (30:20)  
    - Karthik noted filtering is complicated because participant addition dates and submission dates differ.  
    - Applying date filters can cause mismatches in scores since surveys run continuously with rolling responses.  
    - Despite this, the team agreed date filtering is important for monthly and quarterly trend analysis.

- **Export capabilities and confidential reports support deeper analysis** (29:14)  
    - Dashboards allow CSV exports of engagement scores, participation rates, and eNPS but not all driver scores.  
    - Confidential reports provide detailed monthly trend data used by some customers for deeper insights.  
    - Sonia stressed the need to extend export and filtering options to all survey questions for fuller analysis.

- **Dashboard driver cleanup feature planned but timeline pending** (33:55)  
    - The team agreed to add an option to uncheck unused custom drivers to keep dashboards cleaner.  
    - This feature is accepted but delivery depends on effort estimates from Culture Monkey.  
    - Updates on timeline and implementation will follow alongside other enhancement planning.

- **Industry benchmark settings confirmed as account-wide consistent** (33:02)  
    - Benchmark comparisons use the same industry settings as the engagement survey across the entire account.  
    - This consistency helps with meaningful interpretation of scores and trends.

- **Next steps include investigating date filter feasibility and dashboard improvements** (34:55)  
    - Karthik initiated an internal inquiry on adding date range filters to lifecycle analytics dashboards.  
    - The team will provide updates on roadmap feasibility and prioritize this based on user needs.  
    - Sonia emphasized the importance of these features for team reporting autonomy and insight generation.


**Yugi P**
Load 30-day survey file shortly after call completion (05:29)
Provide level of effort estimates for 2026 pulse survey feature enhancements by next morning (14:00)
Enable CSV download option next to lifecycle analytics graphs after this call (29:40)

**Karthik N**
Confirm ability to restrict survey reminders to one reminder at 7 days and limit survey reminder period to 14 days instead of 30 days, and provide confirmation on feasibility this week (24:38)
Investigate and update on feasibility and roadmap for date range filter in lifecycle analytics dashboards (34:55)

**Scott Domes**
Follow up on 30-day survey file loading status on or before February 18 or 19 (07:42)
Track and audit survey response data weekly post-deployment for accuracy and report results to senior management (08:50)

**Kate Burk**
Collaborate with Scott on sub-admin access adjustments and support Lisa on project closeout (11:30)

**Charlie Trinco**
Lead internal prioritization discussion for pulse survey enhancement features informed by Culture Monkey level of effort estimates due tomorrow (12:15)
